summary: Kenneth reviews his weekly covered call portfolio ($1,100-$1,400/week target) with mixed results: profitable on HIMS but significant losses on QuantumCape and NPhase. He emphasizes that covered calls carry real downside risk beyond capped upside, and warns viewers against blindly copying his positions without proper risk tolerance and experience.
takeaways: Initial position sizing: Only allocate capital you're comfortable risking (e.g., $5,700 for QuantumCape despite 8% premium)
Strike selection must be realistic: QuantumCape at $11.50 strike became worthless out-of-money when stock dropped to $8.26
Premium targets: $1,100-$1,400/week on portfolio (up from initial $180/week HIMS goal)
Weekly expiration preference to avoid capital tie-up (avoid 2-week expirations when possible)
Real downside risk exists despite premium collection (15 years experience needed to manage $15k in concurrent risky positions)
Monitor during-week volatility: Buy back losing covered calls or sell stock at loss if thesis breaks, don't just hold
Post losses on QuantumCape and NPhase tied up ~$15k in capital while waiting for potential rebounds

strategy_candidates.change: Implement position sizing rule: limit high-volatility single positions to <5% of portfolio or preset loss-limit buy-back triggers when strike goes >20% out-of-money
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth had QuantumCape drop from $11.47 entry to $8.26 (28% loss) with strike $11.50 becoming unrecoverable; he explicitly states he sizes positions to his risk tolerance but newer traders are blindly copying without this discipline

title: 8/1/25 PART 9.5
